items: Folio mode is here: browse notes in a focused list beside the editor without opening the full file tree
Folio mode can show all notes, a folder, a tag, a person, daily notes, templates, or search results
The local graph now shows shared tag nodes, helping related notes appear as one wider cluster
Property links like project: [[Launch]] now count as real note relationships in backlinks, the graph, and the note info sidebar
Added a Discord link so you can join the community and help shape Glyph
Notes, calendars, databases, AI history, AI profiles, and settings now load and update more consistently
Moving around Glyph should feel faster, with fewer repeated loads and better refreshes after changes
The local graph is smoother and more reliable, with built-in pan and zoom
The graph now has a cleaner full-surface layout with softer nodes, clearer lines, and simpler controls
Graph links now show direction more clearly with arrowheads and better spacing for two-way links
The graph opens with better spacing, clearer node sizes, a dotted background, and a compact legend
Commands, shortcuts, and menu labels now stay in sync across the command palette, settings, and native menus
Renaming or moving a note or folder now updates exact links pointing to it
Link repair now keeps aliases and headings when possible and tells you how many links were updated
Removed extra hover effects that did not add useful feedback
The changelog in About settings is now simpler and more minimal
Removed refresh tab and refresh database buttons because Glyph now refreshes affected views automatically
Extract Selection to Note is now smoother for wikilink-heavy workflows: select text, turn it into a new note, and leave a clean wikilink behind in one focused motion
The editor ribbon now includes Extract to Note next to the link action, making it faster to turn important ideas into connected notes
Several flashier icons across the app have been replaced with minimal, sleeker alternatives for a calmer Glyph workspace
Linked note hover previews are here: hover over a wikilink to quickly see what the note is about
Database search now works inside each view and stays saved with that view
Table and list views can now group notes into clear sections
Database property and value suggestions are cleaner when editing columns and cells
Saved column widths and view settings are now more reliable
Database grouping uses the existing saved view settings, so each view remembers how it is grouped
Backlinks, wikilinks, and linked notes in the sidebar now share the same clean, consistent style
Tag picking and suggestions in table databases are now much more dependable
Board cards have been simplified by removing markdown previews and extra detail text
Property suggestions are simpler now that count-based ranking has been removed
Board cards now use flatter styling, with gradients, lift effects, heavy drag shadows, and extra drop styling removed
Board lane titles and color swatches have cleaner, simpler hover states
Tabs can now be dragged and rearranged, making it easier to organize active notes and views
All Notes in the sidebar now shows the number of notes, so larger spaces are easier to scan at a glance
Number fields from frontmatter can now be sorted in increasing or decreasing order in table databases
Text fields in databases are now more flexible and easier to work with
Task notes are now possible in the editor: Shift+Enter adds quieter detail text under a task, and Cmd+Enter creates the next checkbox task when you are inside those details or a numbered sub-list
Preview toolbar actions for PNG, JPEG, PDF, and other files now use icons and compact accent buttons that match the sidebar New Note button
The sidebar edge has been cleaned up so the old right-side sliver no longer appears
Quick Notes are here: press Hyperkey + Q anywhere on your Mac to capture thoughts the moment they arrive, with a focused flow built for Quick Notes and Scratchpad notes
Smarter editor empty state with a gentler prompt to start writing or press slash to discover commands
Focused folder navigation in the file tree, so you can step into individual folders and keep busy workspaces easier to scan
Hover states across the app have been refined for clearer feedback and a more polished feel
Kanban boards now feel cleaner and more dependable, with UI refinements and columns that stay where you left them
Notes Info Sidebar now uses a transparent treatment to better match the left sidebar and keep the editor feeling calm
Database views now rename correctly, keeping view management smooth and predictable
New status property type for project planning, giving databases a clearer way to track project stages
List view is now available for databases, alongside refreshed view tabs that make switching layouts faster
Kanban cards are now draggable with a much smoother and more reliable board experience
Task progress now uses a vibrant segmented pie chart with a thin status ring, making completion easier to read across the file tree, editor, and database views
Files and folders can now be dragged directly in the file tree for faster organization
Major sidebar UI overhaul with cleaner navigation, sharper spacing, and a calmer workspace feel
Sidebar rendering has been redesigned and tuned for a cleaner feel, with average performance improvements of around 50 to 60 percent
Space names in the sidebar are now better aligned for a cleaner navigation layout
New Note button styling now uses the app accent more clearly
Tags UI has been refined with clearer spacing, contrast, and interaction states
Editing ribbon colors have been inverted for better readability
AI panel input box UI has been cleaned up for a more polished writing flow
YAML handling has been cleaned up for a smoother note editing experience
Removed a large amount of dead code and refactored close to 5000 lines so Glyph stays lighter and easier to maintain
Sidebar note info now refreshes correctly when frontmatter and properties change
Sidebar no longer closes immediately when it should stay open
